title: Minimum Spanning Trees and Network Design · Spiral Review
summary: Connect sites at minimum build cost while explaining redundancy limits. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a minimum-network design and sensitivity brief after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use safe-edge cut arguments to justify minimum-spanning-tree choices.
Run Kruskal with deterministic edge ordering and disjoint-set cycle prevention.
Run Prim with frontier priorities and stale-entry handling.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-dsa-2 algorithm evidence portfolio."""

from dataclasses import dataclass
from heapq import heappop, heappush
from typing import TypeVar

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Cut Property correctness-and-cost proof for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Cut Property assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Connect four sites with weighted edges AB:4, AC:6, BC:5, BD:8, CD:4. Trace representatives or safe edges and state what resilience the result does not guarantee.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Cut Property has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Cut Property precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: The lightest remaining edge is always safe.
